'''Rhys Evans''' (born 1992) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|wg|ce}} or {{rlp|fb}} mostly recently for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He has played for both the [[England national rugby league team|England Knights]] and [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at international level.

He has previously played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Leigh Centurions]], [[Swinton Lions]] and the [[North Wales Crusaders]]. He has also played for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and also on loan from [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] at the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the Super League.

==Background==
Evans was born in [[Bridgend]], Wales.

He is the twin brother of fellow Wales international [[Ben Evans (rugby league)|Ben Evans]].

==Early career==
Both he and his brother were noticed by scouts of numerous Super League clubs while representing their secondary school, [[Brynteg Comprehensive School]], in the National Schools Rugby League final on two separate occasions, 2004 and 2005.

Although he had been brought up in an area dominated by [[rugby union]], and playing junior rugby for [[Tondu RFC]], he and his brother accepted a scholarship with [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] and moved there as teenagers.

He played seven [[Super League]] matches in his first season, scoring a try in his debut game against Leeds.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-04-26 |title=Evans boys on twin track to success at Warrington |url=https://www.the-independent.com/sport/general/rugby-league/evans-boys-on-twin-track-to-success-at-warrington-2275058.html |access-date=2026-03-24 |website=The Independent |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last=Wilson |first=Andy |date=2011-03-05 |title=Warrington dominate Leeds with added bite from Rhys Evans's debut |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/mar/05/warrington-wolves-leeds-rhinos-super-league |access-date=2026-03-24 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}</ref>

==Domestic career==
Evans made his [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] debut in 2010.

In the 2014 season, Evans scored 16 tries playing on the {{rlp|wg}}. However, his game time was restricted in 2015 through multiple injuries.

In 2016, Evans has begun the season playing at {{rlp|ce}}, scoring 2 tries against the [[Catalans Dragons]] in a 30–20 win.

He played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|work=The Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|work=The Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

In June 2017 Evans signed a three-year deal to join Leigh from the start of the 2018 season.<ref name="Leigh Centurions: Rhys Evans and Nick Rawsthorne join Super League side">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40339410|title=Leigh Centurions: Rhys Evans and Nick Rawsthorne join Super League side|publisher=bbc.co.uk|date=20 June 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref>

On 30 September 2020 it was announced that Evans would return to Bradford for the 2021 season, after the 2020 Leeds loan deal ended<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rhys-evans-returns-to-bradford/|title=Evans returns to Bradford|website=loverugbyleague.com|date=30 September 2020|access-date=24 December 2020}}</ref>

He retired from rugby in 2022.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-11-08 |title=Former Bulls, Super League and Wales star announces retirement from rugby league |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23110469.former-bradford-bulls-star-rhys-evans-announces-retirement/ |access-date=2026-03-24 |website=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |language=en}}</ref>

==International career==
Having represented Wales at youth level, Evans made his [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] début in their opening match of the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].

He was selected in the [[Wales 9s]] squad for the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].<ref>{{cite web|date=9 October 2019|title=16-man squad for 9s World Cup revealed|url=https://www.walesrugbyleague.co.uk/article/16722/-man-squad-for-s-world-cup-revealed|publisher=Wales RL|access-date=31 October 2019}}</ref>
